Do define clear module APIs as if they were remote service contracts because this preserves future extractability
Do start modular from day one because retrofitting modularity into a tangled monolith is far more expensive
dos_zh: 使用自动化工具强制模块边界，因为仅靠人工自律会随时间侵蚀边界
为每个模块分配独立的数据模式或分区，因为共享数据库是产生隐藏耦合的最快途径
像远程服务契约一样定义清晰的模块API，因为这保留了未来可提取性
从第一天就采用模块化设计，因为在混乱的单体中补装模块化代价要高得多
donts: Don't allow modules to share domain objects directly because it creates invisible coupling that defeats the purpose of boundaries
Don't skip boundary enforcement tooling because without automated checks, modules will gradually become entangled
Don't treat the modular monolith as a permanent end state if growth demands because it should remain ready for selective extraction
Don't over-decompose into too many tiny modules because excessive granularity in a monolith adds complexity without distributed benefits
donts_zh: 不要允许模块直接共享领域对象，因为这会产生隐形耦合，违背边界的初衷
不要跳过边界强制工具，因为没有自动化检查，模块会逐渐变得纠缠不清
不要在增长需要时仍将模块化单体视为永久终态，它应保持随时可选择性提取的状态
不要过度拆分为过多小模块，因为单体中过细的粒度只增加复杂性而无分布式收益
case_study: Shopify migrated its massive Ruby on Rails monolith into a modular monolith architecture using component-based design with enforced boundaries. Instead of breaking into microservices, they introduced a componentization framework that divided the application into well-defined modules with explicit dependencies. This approach reduced build times, enabled parallel team development, and maintained the simplicity of single-process deployment while serving millions of merchants globally.
case_study_zh: Shopify将其庞大的Ruby on Rails单体迁移为基于组件设计的模块化单体架构，并强制执行边界。他们没有拆分为微服务，而是引入组件化框架，将应用划分为具有明确依赖关系的模块。这种方法缩短了构建时间，支持了团队并行开发，并在服务全球数百万商户的同时保持了单进程部署的简洁性。
when_not_to_use: When independent scaling of specific components is a hard requirement that a single deployment unit cannot satisfy
When teams are geographically distributed and need fully independent deployment pipelines for each service
When the application is already well-decomposed into microservices and reverting to a monolith offers no clear benefit
when_not_to_use_zh: 当特定组件的独立扩展是硬性需求，单一部署单元无法满足时
当团队地理分布需要为每个服务提供完全独立的部署管道时
当应用已经良好地拆分为微服务，回归单体没有明显收益时

name: Space-Based Architecture
desc: Distributed in-memory computing pattern achieving extreme scalability by eliminating central database bottlenecks
desc_zh: 通过消除中央数据库瓶颈，利用分布式内存计算实现极致可扩展性的架构模式
steps: Identify the processing units: self-contained modules that include business logic, in-memory data grid, and optional async persistence
Design the virtualized middleware layer with messaging grid, data grid, processing grid, and deployment manager
Partition data across processing units using a distributed in-memory data grid for low-latency read/write operations
Implement asynchronous data persistence: write to in-memory grid first, then replicate to database through queue-based writers
Configure the deployment manager to dynamically start and stop processing unit instances based on load conditions
steps_zh: 识别处理单元：包含业务逻辑、内存数据网格和可选异步持久化的自包含模块
设计虚拟化中间件层，包含消息网格、数据网格、处理网格和部署管理器
使用分布式内存数据网格在处理单元间分区数据，实现低延迟读写操作
实现异步数据持久化：先写入内存网格，再通过基于队列的写入器复制到数据库
配置部署管理器，根据负载条件动态启动和停止处理单元实例
origin_author: Richards & Ford, 2020; rooted in tuple-space concepts (Gelernter, 1985)
origin_source: Fundamentals of Software Architecture (Richards & Ford, 2020); Software Architecture in Practice (Bass et al., 2021)
origin_source_zh: 《软件架构基础》（Richards & Ford, 2020）；《软件架构实践》（Bass等, 2021）
when_to_use: When the system must handle extreme spikes in concurrent user load such as concert ticketing or flash sales
When the central database has become the primary scalability bottleneck despite query optimization and caching
When ultra-low latency is required and in-memory processing must replace traditional database round-trips
When elastic scalability is needed to dynamically add and remove processing capacity on demand
when_to_use_zh: 当系统必须处理极端的并发用户负载峰值，如演唱会售票或限时抢购时
当中央数据库在查询优化和缓存之后仍然是主要的可扩展性瓶颈时
当需要超低延迟、必须用内存处理替代传统数据库往返时
当需要弹性扩展以按需动态增减处理能力时
core_concepts: Processing unit: Self-contained deployment unit bundling application logic with an in-memory data grid and optional persistence
Virtualized middleware: Infrastructure layer providing messaging, data replication, request routing, and deployment management
Data replication: In-memory data synchronized across processing units so each instance has the data it needs locally
Asynchronous persistence: Database writes happen asynchronously via queues, decoupling application speed from database speed
Elastic scaling: Dynamic instantiation and removal of processing units based on real-time demand, managed by the deployment manager
core_concepts_zh: 处理单元：将应用逻辑与内存数据网格和可选持久化打包的自包含部署单元
虚拟化中间件：提供消息传递、数据复制、请求路由和部署管理的基础设施层
数据复制：内存数据在处理单元间同步，使每个实例在本地拥有所需数据
异步持久化：数据库写入通过队列异步完成，将应用速度与数据库速度解耦
弹性扩展：根据实时需求动态创建和移除处理单元实例，由部署管理器管理
timeline: David Gelernter introduces the tuple-space concept in Linda, the intellectual ancestor of space-based computing
GigaSpaces and Oracle Coherence commercialize in-memory data grid technology for enterprise use
Space-based architecture gains traction for high-frequency trading and real-time bidding platforms
Apache Ignite and Hazelcast emerge as open-source in-memory computing platforms supporting the pattern
Richards & Ford formalize space-based architecture as a distinct style in Fundamentals of Software Architecture
timeline_zh: David Gelernter在Linda语言中引入元组空间概念，成为基于空间计算的思想源头
GigaSpaces和Oracle Coherence将内存数据网格技术商业化用于企业场景
基于空间的架构在高频交易和实时竞价平台中获得广泛应用
Apache Ignite和Hazelcast作为开源内存计算平台涌现，支持该模式
Richards与Ford在《软件架构基础》中将基于空间的架构正式化为独立风格
dos: Do design processing units to be stateless or use replicated in-memory grids because this enables true horizontal scaling
Do implement robust data replication strategies because data inconsistency between processing units can cause subtle bugs
Do use asynchronous persistence queues with guaranteed delivery because data loss during the async write window is the primary risk
Do monitor memory usage per processing unit because in-memory data grids can consume significant heap space under load
dos_zh: 将处理单元设计为无状态或使用复制的内存网格，因为这能实现真正的水平扩展
实现健壮的数据复制策略，因为处理单元间的数据不一致会导致微妙的缺陷
使用具有保证交付的异步持久化队列，因为异步写入窗口中的数据丢失是主要风险
监控每个处理单元的内存使用，因为内存数据网格在负载下可消耗大量堆空间
donts: Don't rely on a central database for real-time reads because it reintroduces the bottleneck the pattern is designed to eliminate
Don't ignore the eventual consistency implications because the async persistence model means the database may lag behind the in-memory state
Don't use this pattern for simple CRUD applications because the operational complexity far exceeds the scalability benefits for low-traffic systems
Don't assume infinite memory because data partitioning and eviction strategies are essential for sustainable operation
donts_zh: 不要依赖中央数据库进行实时读取，因为这会重新引入该模式旨在消除的瓶颈
不要忽视最终一致性的影响，因为异步持久化模型意味着数据库可能落后于内存状态
不要将此模式用于简单的CRUD应用，因为对于低流量系统，运维复杂性远超可扩展性收益
不要假设内存无限，数据分区和逐出策略对可持续运行至关重要
case_study: TicketMaster adopted space-based architecture principles to handle the extreme load spikes during major concert on-sales, where millions of users simultaneously compete for limited tickets. By processing transactions in distributed in-memory grids with asynchronous database persistence, they eliminated the central database as a bottleneck. This allowed them to handle peak loads exceeding 10x normal traffic without degraded response times, reducing failed transactions during high-demand events by over 90%.
case_study_zh: TicketMaster采用基于空间的架构原则来应对大型演唱会开售时的极端负载峰值——数百万用户同时争抢有限的门票。通过在分布式内存网格中处理事务并异步持久化到数据库，他们消除了中央数据库瓶颈。这使他们能够在不降低响应时间的情况下处理超过正常流量10倍的峰值负载，高需求事件期间的失败交易减少了90%以上。
when_not_to_use: Applications with low to moderate traffic where the complexity of in-memory grids and async persistence is unjustified
Systems requiring strong transactional consistency where eventual consistency from async persistence is unacceptable
Budget-constrained environments where the cost of large in-memory infrastructure cannot be justified
when_not_to_use_zh: 低至中等流量的应用，内存网格和异步持久化的复杂性不合理
需要强事务一致性的系统，异步持久化带来的最终一致性不可接受
预算有限的环境，大规模内存基础设施的成本无法合理化

name: Pipe and Filter Architecture
desc: Data processing architecture that chains independent transformation stages connected by data pipes
desc_zh: 通过数据管道连接独立转换阶段的数据处理架构模式
steps: Decompose the overall data processing task into discrete, independent transformation stages (filters) with single responsibilities
Define the data format flowing through pipes: choose a uniform interface so any filter can connect to any other filter
Implement each filter as a self-contained component that reads from an input pipe, processes data, and writes to an output pipe
Connect filters via pipes that handle data buffering, backpressure, and optional parallelization between stages
Compose the pipeline by arranging filters in the desired sequence, testing each filter independently before integration
steps_zh: 将整体数据处理任务分解为离散的、独立的转换阶段（过滤器），每个阶段承担单一职责
定义通过管道流动的数据格式：选择统一接口使任何过滤器都能与其他过滤器连接
将每个过滤器实现为自包含组件，从输入管道读取数据、处理并写入输出管道
通过管道连接过滤器，管道处理数据缓冲、背压和阶段间的可选并行化
按所需顺序编排过滤器组成管道，在集成前独立测试每个过滤器
origin_author: Doug McIlroy, 1964 (Unix pipes); formalized by Shaw & Garlan, 1996
origin_source: Software Architecture: Perspectives on an Emerging Discipline (Shaw & Garlan, 1996); Fundamentals of Software Architecture (Richards & Ford, 2020)
origin_source_zh: 《软件架构：新兴学科的视角》（Shaw & Garlan, 1996）；《软件架构基础》（Richards & Ford, 2020）
when_to_use: When processing data through a sequence of well-defined transformation steps such as ETL pipelines or stream processing
When each transformation stage should be independently developed, tested, and potentially reused across different pipelines
When building AI/ML inference pipelines that chain preprocessing, model execution, and postprocessing stages
When the processing flow benefits from parallel execution of independent stages to improve throughput
when_to_use_zh: 当数据需要经过一系列定义良好的转换步骤处理时，如ETL管道或流处理
当每个转换阶段应该独立开发、测试，并可能在不同管道间复用时
当构建链接预处理、模型执行和后处理阶段的AI/ML推理管道时
当处理流程受益于独立阶段的并行执行以提高吞吐量时
core_concepts: Filter: An independent processing component that transforms input data to output data without knowledge of adjacent filters
Pipe: A connector that transfers data between filters, potentially providing buffering, ordering, and backpressure mechanisms
Uniform interface: All filters share the same input/output data contract, enabling arbitrary composition and reordering
Composability: Pipelines can be assembled, extended, or modified by adding, removing, or rearranging filters without changing other components
Incremental processing: Data flows through the pipeline piece by piece rather than in batch, enabling streaming and reduced memory footprint
core_concepts_zh: 过滤器：独立的处理组件，将输入数据转换为输出数据，无需了解相邻过滤器
管道：在过滤器间传输数据的连接器，可提供缓冲、排序和背压机制
统一接口：所有过滤器共享相同的输入/输出数据契约，支持任意组合和重排
可组合性：通过增加、移除或重排过滤器来组装、扩展或修改管道，无需更改其他组件
增量处理：数据逐片流经管道而非批量处理，支持流式处理并降低内存占用
timeline: Doug McIlroy proposes the concept of connecting programs like garden hoses, leading to Unix pipes
Unix pipes (|) are implemented by Ken Thompson, making pipe-and-filter the first widely used architecture pattern
Shaw & Garlan formalize pipe-and-filter as a core architectural style in their foundational textbook
Apache Kafka and modern stream processing frameworks bring pipe-and-filter principles to distributed systems
Richards & Ford describe pipe-and-filter as a fundamental topology in Fundamentals of Software Architecture
timeline_zh: Doug McIlroy提出像连接花园水管一样连接程序的概念，催生了Unix管道
Ken Thompson实现Unix管道（|），使管道与过滤器成为首个广泛使用的架构模式
Shaw与Garlan在其奠基性教材中将管道与过滤器正式化为核心架构风格
Apache Kafka和现代流处理框架将管道与过滤器原则引入分布式系统
Richards与Ford在《软件架构基础》中将管道与过滤器描述为基础拓扑结构
dos: Do keep filters independent and stateless because shared state between filters defeats the pattern's composability advantage
Do define a uniform data interface between filters because incompatible formats create brittle, hard-to-reconfigure pipelines
Do implement backpressure mechanisms because without them a fast producer will overwhelm a slow consumer and cause data loss or memory exhaustion
Do design filters for single responsibility because multi-purpose filters reduce reusability and complicate testing
dos_zh: 保持过滤器独立和无状态，因为过滤器间的共享状态会破坏模式的可组合性优势
在过滤器间定义统一的数据接口，因为不兼容的格式会产生脆弱、难以重配置的管道
实现背压机制，因为没有背压时快速生产者会压垂慢速消费者导致数据丢失或内存耗尽
为单一职责设计过滤器，因为多用途过滤器降低可复用性并增加测试复杂度
donts: Don't build filters that depend on the ordering or identity of adjacent filters because it destroys the ability to freely compose pipelines
Don't use pipe-and-filter for interactive request-response workflows because the sequential pass-through model adds unnecessary latency
Don't ignore error handling between stages because a failure in one filter can silently corrupt the entire downstream pipeline
Don't over-fragment into too many tiny filters because excessive granularity introduces per-hop overhead that degrades performance
donts_zh: 不要构建依赖相邻过滤器顺序或身份的过滤器，因为这会破坏自由组合管道的能力
不要将管道与过滤器用于交互式请求-响应工作流，因为顺序透传模型增加不必要的延迟
不要忽视阶段间的错误处理，因为一个过滤器的故障可能无声地污染整个下游管道
不要过度碎片化为过多小型过滤器，因为过细的粒度引入逐跳开销降低性能
case_study: Netflix employs pipe-and-filter architecture extensively in its data engineering platform. Their video encoding pipeline processes raw uploaded content through a series of independent filters: format validation, transcoding to multiple resolutions, quality analysis, and DRM packaging. Each filter operates independently and can be scaled, updated, or replaced without affecting others. This architecture processes millions of hours of video content while allowing the team to optimize individual stages independently.
case_study_zh: Netflix在其数据工程平台中广泛采用管道与过滤器架构。其视频编码管道通过一系列独立过滤器处理上传的原始内容：格式验证、多分辨率转码、质量分析和DRM打包。每个过滤器独立运行，可单独扩展、更新或替换而不影响其他组件。该架构处理数百万小时的视频内容，同时允许团队独立优化各个阶段。
when_not_to_use: Interactive applications requiring bidirectional communication where the unidirectional data flow model is a poor fit
Workloads where the overhead of data serialization and transfer between filters exceeds the processing time within filters
Systems where filters need to share significant state, making the independence assumption impractical
when_not_to_use_zh: 需要双向通信的交互式应用，单向数据流模型不适用
过滤器间数据序列化和传输开销超过过滤器内处理时间的工作负载
过滤器需要共享大量状态、使独立性假设不切实际的系统

name: Architecture Fitness Functions
desc: Automated, objective checks that verify architecture characteristics are maintained as the system evolves
desc_zh: 自动化的客观检查机制，验证系统演进过程中架构特性是否得到维护
steps: Identify the critical architecture characteristics (performance, modularity, security, coupling) that must be preserved
Define measurable thresholds for each characteristic — for example, response time under 200ms at the 95th percentile
Implement automated fitness functions as tests: unit-level (dependency checks), integration-level (latency), and holistic (deployment frequency)
Integrate fitness functions into the CI/CD pipeline so they run on every commit or at scheduled intervals
Review and evolve fitness functions periodically because architecture goals shift as the system and business requirements change
steps_zh: 识别必须保持的关键架构特性（性能、模块性、安全性、耦合度）
为每个特性定义可衡量的阈值——例如，95百分位响应时间低于200毫秒
将适应度函数实现为自动化测试：单元级（依赖检查）、集成级（延迟）和整体级（部署频率）
将适应度函数集成到CI/CD管道中，在每次提交或定期运行
定期审查和演进适应度函数，因为架构目标随系统和业务需求变化而调整
origin_author: Neal Ford, Rebecca Parsons & Patrick Kua, 2017
origin_source: Building Evolutionary Architectures (Ford, Parsons & Kua, 2017); Fundamentals of Software Architecture (Richards & Ford, 2020)
origin_source_zh: 《构建演进式架构》（Ford、Parsons与Kua, 2017）；《软件架构基础》（Richards & Ford, 2020）
when_to_use: When architecture degradation is a recurring problem and the team needs automated guardrails to prevent erosion
When multiple teams contribute to a shared codebase and consistent architecture compliance must be enforced
When the system must evolve rapidly while preserving key quality attributes like performance or modularity
When governance processes are too slow or subjective and need to be replaced with objective, automated checks
when_to_use_zh: 当架构退化是反复出现的问题，团队需要自动化护栏防止侵蚀时
当多个团队贡献到共享代码库，必须强制执行一致的架构合规性时
当系统必须快速演进同时保持性能或模块性等关键质量属性时
当治理流程过慢或主观，需要用客观的自动化检查替代时
core_concepts: Fitness function: An objective, automated assessment that measures how well the system preserves a specific architecture characteristic
Atomic fitness function: Tests a single characteristic in isolation, such as cyclic dependency detection or response time benchmarks
Holistic fitness function: Evaluates system-wide properties that emerge from multiple components working together, such as overall scalability
Triggered vs. continuous: Triggered functions run in CI pipelines on each change; continuous functions monitor production characteristics in real time
Evolutionary architecture: The overarching philosophy that architecture should support guided, incremental change rather than resist it
core_concepts_zh: 适应度函数：客观的自动化评估，衡量系统在多大程度上保持了特定架构特性
原子适应度函数：隔离测试单个特性，如循环依赖检测或响应时间基准
整体适应度函数：评估由多个组件协同工作产生的系统级属性，如整体可扩展性
触发式与持续式：触发式在CI管道中每次变更时运行；持续式实时监控生产环境特性
演进式架构：总体理念——架构应支持有引导的增量变化，而非抵制变化
timeline: Ford, Parsons & Kua introduce fitness functions in Building Evolutionary Architectures
ArchUnit for Java emerges as a key tool for implementing structural fitness functions
ThoughtWorks Technology Radar features fitness functions as a recommended technique
Richards & Ford integrate fitness functions into their architecture characteristics framework in Fundamentals of Software Architecture
Fitness functions expand to include AI-assisted code analysis and architecture drift detection in CI/CD platforms
timeline_zh: Ford、Parsons与Kua在《构建演进式架构》中引入适应度函数概念
Java版ArchUnit作为实现结构适应度函数的关键工具出现
ThoughtWorks技术雷达将适应度函数列为推荐技术
Richards与Ford在《软件架构基础》中将适应度函数整合到架构特性框架中
适应度函数扩展到包括CI/CD平台中AI辅助的代码分析和架构漂移检测
dos: Do tie each fitness function to a specific, measurable architecture characteristic because vague goals produce meaningless tests
Do run fitness functions in CI/CD because architecture drift caught late in the process is exponentially more expensive to fix
Do evolve fitness functions as requirements change because outdated thresholds provide false confidence in architecture health
Do start with the most critical characteristics first because comprehensive coverage from day one is impractical and overwhelming
dos_zh: 将每个适应度函数绑定到具体的、可衡量的架构特性，因为模糊的目标产生无意义的测试
在CI/CD中运行适应度函数，因为晚发现的架构漂移修复成本呈指数增长
随需求变化演进适应度函数，因为过时的阈值会对架构健康状态产生虚假信心
从最关键的特性开始，因为从第一天就全面覆盖不切实际且令人不堪重负
donts: Don't create fitness functions without clear pass/fail thresholds because subjective assessments defeat the purpose of automation
Don't test only at the code level because many architecture characteristics (latency, scalability) can only be validated at integration or system level
Don't treat fitness functions as a substitute for architecture thinking because they verify decisions but don't make them
Don't let fitness function failures become normalized because ignoring red builds erodes the entire governance mechanism
donts_zh: 不要创建没有明确通过/失败阈值的适应度函数，因为主观评估违背了自动化的目的
不要仅在代码级别测试，因为许多架构特性（延迟、可扩展性）只能在集成或系统级别验证
不要将适应度函数视为架构思考的替代品，因为它们验证决策但不制定决策
不要让适应度函数失败成为常态，因为忽视红色构建会侵蚀整个治理机制
case_study: ThoughtWorks pioneered architecture fitness functions across its consulting engagements. On a large financial services platform, they implemented fitness functions to enforce modularity (no circular dependencies between modules), performance (API response times under SLA thresholds), and security (no new vulnerable dependency introductions). Running these in CI/CD caught over 70% of architecture violations before they reached code review, transforming architecture governance from a periodic manual audit into a continuous automated process.
case_study_zh: ThoughtWorks在其咨询项目中率先推广架构适应度函数。在一个大型金融服务平台上，他们实现了适应度函数来强制模块性（模块间无循环依赖）、性能（API响应时间低于SLA阈值）和安全性（不引入新的易受攻击依赖）。在CI/CD中运行这些函数在代码审查前就捕获了70%以上的架构违规，将架构治理从定期人工审计转变为持续自动化流程。
when_not_to_use: Very early-stage prototypes where architecture characteristics are still being discovered and thresholds are meaningless
Small personal projects where the overhead of maintaining fitness functions exceeds the risk of architecture degradation
Environments without CI/CD infrastructure where fitness functions cannot be automated and run consistently
when_not_to_use_zh: 架构特性仍在探索中且阈值无意义的早期原型阶段
维护适应度函数的开销超过架构退化风险的小型个人项目
没有CI/CD基础设施、无法自动化和一致运行适应度函数的环境

name: Decision Matrix (Weighted Scoring)
desc: Systematic evaluation method for comparing architecture alternatives against weighted criteria
desc_zh: 通过加权标准系统化比较架构备选方案的评估方法
steps: List all viable architecture alternatives as columns and all evaluation criteria (scalability, cost, complexity, team skill) as rows
Assign weights to each criterion based on stakeholder priorities, ensuring weights sum to a consistent total (e.g., 100%)
Score each alternative against each criterion on a uniform scale (e.g., 1-5), using objective data where possible and expert judgment where necessary
Calculate weighted scores by multiplying each raw score by its criterion weight, then sum the weighted scores for each alternative
Analyze the results: compare totals, examine sensitivity by adjusting key weights, and document the rationale for the final selection in an ADR
steps_zh: 将所有可行的架构备选方案列为列，所有评估标准（可扩展性、成本、复杂性、团队技能）列为行
根据利益相关者优先级为每个标准分配权重，确保权重总和一致（如100%）
用统一量表（如1-5分）对每个备选方案的每个标准打分，尽可能使用客观数据，必要时用专家判断
计算加权得分：将每个原始分数乘以其标准权重，然后汇总每个备选方案的加权得分
分析结果：比较总分，通过调整关键权重进行敏感性分析，并在ADR中记录最终选择的理由
origin_author: Stuart Pugh, 1991 (Pugh Matrix); adapted for software architecture by Richards & Ford, 2020
origin_source: Total Design (Pugh, 1991); Fundamentals of Software Architecture (Richards & Ford, 2020); Software Architecture in Practice (Bass et al., 2021)
origin_source_zh: 《全面设计》（Pugh, 1991）；《软件架构基础》（Richards & Ford, 2020）；《软件架构实践》（Bass等, 2021）
when_to_use: When choosing between multiple architecture alternatives and stakeholders need a transparent, defensible comparison
When architecture decisions involve competing quality attributes and the relative importance of each must be made explicit
When the team needs to move beyond gut-feel decisions and adopt a structured evaluation process
When documenting architecture trade-offs for compliance, governance, or future reference
when_to_use_zh: 当在多个架构备选方案间选择，利益相关者需要透明、可辩护的比较时
当架构决策涉及竞争性质量属性，需要明确每个属性的相对重要性时
当团队需要超越直觉决策，采用结构化评估流程时
当需要为合规、治理或未来参考记录架构权衡时
core_concepts: Criteria identification: Selecting the quality attributes, constraints, and business factors that will drive the architecture decision
Weight assignment: Quantifying the relative importance of each criterion based on stakeholder priorities and project context
Scoring consistency: Using a uniform scale and clear rubrics so that scores are comparable and reproducible across evaluators
Sensitivity analysis: Testing how the final ranking changes when key weights are adjusted, revealing how robust the decision is
Transparency and traceability: The matrix provides an auditable artifact showing exactly why a particular alternative was selected
core_concepts_zh: 标准识别：选择驱动架构决策的质量属性、约束条件和业务因素
权重分配：根据利益相关者优先级和项目上下文量化每个标准的相对重要性
评分一致性：使用统一量表和明确的评分规则，使分数在评估者间可比较和可复现
敏感性分析：测试调整关键权重时最终排名如何变化，揭示决策的稳健程度
透明性与可追溯性：矩阵提供可审计的产物，清晰展示为何选择某个特定方案
timeline: Stuart Pugh publishes Total Design, introducing the Pugh Matrix for systematic concept selection
Bass, Clements & Kazman incorporate structured trade-off analysis into Software Architecture in Practice
Decision matrices become standard practice in enterprise architecture review boards
Richards & Ford present weighted scoring as a key architecture decision technique in Fundamentals of Software Architecture
AI-assisted decision matrices emerge, using LLMs to help identify criteria, suggest weights, and synthesize scoring rationale
timeline_zh: Stuart Pugh出版《全面设计》，引入Pugh矩阵用于系统化概念选择
Bass、Clements与Kazman在《软件架构实践》中纳入结构化权衡分析
决策矩阵成为企业架构评审委员会的标准实践
Richards与Ford在《软件架构基础》中将加权评分作为关键架构决策技术呈现
AI辅助决策矩阵涌现，使用LLM帮助识别标准、建议权重和综合评分理由
dos: Do involve multiple stakeholders in weight assignment because single-perspective weighting introduces hidden bias
Do use objective data for scoring wherever possible because subjective scores without evidence are easily disputed
Do perform sensitivity analysis on the top-weighted criteria because small weight changes can flip the final ranking
Do document the scoring rationale for each cell because raw numbers without explanation lose their value over time
dos_zh: 让多个利益相关者参与权重分配，因为单一视角的权重会引入隐藏偏见
尽可能使用客观数据评分，因为没有证据支撑的主观分数容易引发争议
对权重最高的标准进行敏感性分析，因为微小的权重变化可能翻转最终排名
记录每个单元格的评分理由，因为没有解释的原始数字会随时间失去价值
donts: Don't assign equal weights to all criteria because it eliminates the purpose of prioritization and often produces inconclusive results
Don't use the matrix to justify a predetermined choice because reverse-engineering weights to match a desired outcome destroys credibility
Don't skip criteria that are hard to quantify (team morale, learning curve) because ignoring them doesn't eliminate their impact on success
Don't treat the highest-scoring alternative as automatically correct because the matrix informs the decision but does not make it
donts_zh: 不要给所有标准分配相同权重，因为这消除了优先级排序的意义，常产生无结论结果
不要用矩阵来合理化预设选择，因为逆向调整权重以匹配预期结果会破坏可信度
不要跳过难以量化的标准（团队士气、学习曲线），因为忽略它们不会消除其对成功的影响
不要将最高分的备选方案视为自动正确，因为矩阵为决策提供信息但不代替决策
case_study: Zalando used weighted decision matrices when migrating from a monolithic Java application to a microservices architecture. For each bounded context being extracted, they evaluated three alternatives (pure microservice, modular monolith component, serverless function) against criteria weighted by the domain team: independent deployability (30%), operational complexity (25%), team autonomy (20%), latency requirements (15%), and cost (10%). This systematic approach prevented a one-size-fits-all migration and resulted in a heterogeneous architecture where each domain chose the pattern best suited to its specific constraints.
case_study_zh: Zalando在从单体Java应用迁移到微服务架构时使用了加权决策矩阵。对于每个被提取的限界上下文，他们评估三种备选方案（纯微服务、模块化单体组件、无服务器函数），对照由领域团队加权的标准：独立部署能力（30%）、运维复杂性（25%）、团队自主权（20%）、延迟要求（15%）和成本（10%）。这种系统化方法避免了千篇一律的迁移，产生了异构架构，每个领域选择最适合其特定约束的模式。
when_not_to_use: When the decision is straightforward with an obviously superior alternative, making the matrix overhead unnecessary
When criteria cannot be meaningfully weighted because all factors are equally important or importance is genuinely unknown
When rapid prototyping is more valuable than upfront analysis and the decision can be cheaply reversed
when_not_to_use_zh: 当决策直截了当、有明显优越的备选方案时，矩阵的开销不必要
当标准无法有意义地加权，因为所有因素同等重要或重要性确实未知时
当快速原型比前期分析更有价值且决策可以低成本撤销时
